info.title: Render Public API
info.description: Manage everything about your Render services

tags.name: Audit Logs
tags.description:
[Audit Logs](https://render.com/docs/audit-logs) allow you to retrieve audit logs for workspaces and organizations.
These logs provide a trail of actions and changes made to your resources.

tags.name: Blueprints
tags.description:
[Blueprints](https://render.com/docs/infrastructure-as-code) allow you to define your resources in a `render.yaml` file and automatically sync changes to your Render services.
The API gives control over how your Blueprints are used to create and manage resources.

tags.name: Custom Domains
tags.description: [Custom Domains](https://render.com/docs/custom-domains) allow you to associate domain names with your Render services.

tags.name: Dedicated IPs
tags.description: [Dedicated IPs](https://render.com/docs/dedicated-ips) provide static, outbound IP addresses for your Render services.

tags.name: Deploys
tags.description:
[Deploys](https://render.com/docs/deploys) are the process of updating your service with new code or configuration.
These endpoints allow you to retrieve data on the deploys of your services
as well as trigger new deploys and rollbacks. Most changes to your service
will require a new deploy to take effect.

To create and manage services, use the `Services` endpoints.

tags.name: Disks
tags.description: [Disks](https://render.com/docs/disks) allow you to attach persistent storage to your services.

tags.name: Environment Groups
tags.description: Collections of environment variables and secret files that can be shared between multiple services

tags.name: Events
tags.description: View events for a service, postgres or key value

tags.name: Key Value
tags.description: [Key Value](https://render.com/docs/key-value) allows you to interact with your Render Key Value instances.

tags.name: Logs
tags.description:
[Logs](https://render.com/docs/logging) allow you to retrieve logs for your services, Postgres databases, and redis instances.
You can query for logs or subscribe to logs in real-time via a websocket.

tags.name: Maintenance
tags.description: The `Maintenance` endpoints allow you to retrieve the latest maintenance runs for your Render services. You can also reschedule maintenance or trigger it to start immediately.

tags.name: Metrics
tags.description: The `Metrics` endpoints allow you to retrieve metrics for your services, Postgres databases, and redis instances.

tags.name: Notification Settings
tags.description:
[Notification Settings](https://render.com/docs/notifications) allow you to configure which notifications you want to receive, and
where you will receive them.

tags.name: One-Off Jobs
tags.description: [One-off jobs](https://render.com/docs/one-off-jobs) are standalone tasks that run to completion using the most recent successful build of an existing service.

tags.name: Postgres
tags.description:
[Postgres](https://render.com/docs/postgresql) endpoints enable you to interact with your Render Postgres databases.
You can manage databases, exports, recoveries, and failovers.

tags.name: Projects & Environments
tags.description: Collections of services and other resources organized by environment (staging, production, etc.)

tags.name: Registry Credentials
tags.description: [Registry Credentials](https://render.com/docs/deploying-an-image#credentials-for-private-images) allow you to manage credentials for private Docker images.

tags.name: Services
tags.description: [Services](https://render.com/docs/service-types) allow you to manage your web services, private services, background workers, cron jobs, and static sites.

tags.name: Services - Cron Jobs
tags.description: [Cron Jobs](https://render.com/docs/cronjobs) allow you to interact with runs of your cron jobs.

tags.name: Services - Headers
tags.description: Use HTTP headers to inject response headers in static site responses. You can also use wildcards like /path/* to add headers to responses for all matching request paths.

tags.name: Services - Routes
tags.description: Add Redirect or Rewrite Rules to modify requests to your site without writing code. You can use URL parameters to capture path segments, and wildcards to redirect everything under a given path.

tags.name: Users
tags.description: The `User` endpoints allow you to retrieve information about the authenticated user

tags.name: Workspaces
tags.description: The `Workspaces` endpoints supply more information about the workspaces that your API key has access to. This category was previously called `Owners`, as reflected by endpoint paths.

tags.name: Webhooks
tags.description: [Webhooks](https://render.com/docs/webhooks) allow you to manage your Render webhook configuration.

tags.name: Workflows (Beta)
tags.description: Create and manage [Render Workflows](https://render.com/docs/workflows) services. Workflows are in public beta.

tags.name: Workflow Tasks (Beta)
tags.description: Run and manage tasks as part of [Render Workflows](https://render.com/docs/workflows). Workflows are in public beta.

tags.name: Redis (Deprecated)
tags.description: [Redis](https://render.com/docs/redis) allows you to interact with your Render Redis instances. This API is deprecated in favor of the Key Value API.

get.description: List services matching the provided filters. If no filters are provided, returns all services you have permissions to view.

post.description: Creates a new Render service in the specified workspace with the specified configuration.

get.description: List Postgres instances matching the provided filters. If no filters are provided, all Postgres instances are returned.
Retrieve connection info for a Postgres instance by ID. Connection info includes sensitive information.
List all environment variables for the service with the provided ID.

put.description: Replace all environment variables for a service with the provided list of environment variables.

generateValue.description: If true, Render generates a strong random value for this environment variable on creation. Cannot be combined with `value`.

get.description: List a particular service's redirect/rewrite rules that match the provided filters. If no filters are provided, all rules for the service are returned.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Filter for the type of route rule

parameters.name: source
parameters.description: Filter for the source path of the route

parameters.name: destination
parameters.description: Filter for the destination path of the route

post.description: Add redirect/rewrite rules to the service with the provided ID.

patch.description:
Update the priority for a particular redirect/rewrite rule.

To apply redirect/rewrite rules to an incoming request, Render starts from the rule with priority `0` and applies the first encountered rule that matches the request's path (if any).

Render increments the priority of other rules by `1` as necessary to make space for the updated rule.

put.description:
Replace all redirect/rewrite rules for a particular service with the provided list.

**This deletes all existing redirect/rewrite rules for the service that aren't included in the request.**

Rule priority is assigned according to list order (the first rule in the list has the highest priority).

get.description: List deploys matching the provided filters. If no filters are provided, all deploys for the service are returned.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Filter for deploys with the specified statuses

parameters.name: createdBefore
parameters.description: Filter for deploys created before a certain time (specified as an ISO 8601 timestamp)

parameters.name: createdAfter
parameters.description: Filter for deploys created after a certain time (specified as an ISO 8601 timestamp)

parameters.name: updatedBefore
parameters.description: Filter for deploys updated before a certain time (specified as an ISO 8601 timestamp)

parameters.name: updatedAfter
parameters.description: Filter for deploys updated after a certain time (specified as an ISO 8601 timestamp)

parameters.name: finishedBefore
parameters.description: Filter for deploys finished before a certain time (specified as an ISO 8601 timestamp)

parameters.name: finishedAfter
parameters.description: Filter for deploys finished after a certain time (specified as an ISO 8601 timestamp)

post.description: Trigger a deploy for the service with the provided ID.

clearCache.description: If `clear`, Render clears the service's build cache before deploying. This can be useful if you're experiencing issues with your build.

commitId.description:
The SHA of a specific Git commit to deploy for a service. Defaults to the latest commit on the service's connected branch.

Note that deploying a specific commit with this endpoint does not disable autodeploys for the service.

You can toggle autodeploys for your service with the [Update service](https://api-docs.render.com/reference/update-service) endpoint or in the Render Dashboard.

Not supported for cron jobs.

imageUrl.description:
The URL of the image to deploy for an image-backed service.

The host, repository, and image name all must match the currently configured image for the service.

deployMode.description:
Deployment mode controlling build and deploy behavior.

Defaults to `build_and_deploy` when not specified.

**Validation:** `deploy_mode` cannot be combined with `commitId` or `imageUrl` or `clearCache`.

get.description: Retrieve the details of a particular deploy for a particular service.

List logs matching the provided filters. Logs are paginated by start and end timestamps.
There are more logs to fetch if `hasMore` is true in the response. Provide the `nextStartTime`
and `nextEndTime` timestamps as the `startTime` and `endTime` query parameters to fetch the next page of logs.

You can query for logs across multiple resources, but all resources must be in the same region and belong to the same owner.

parameters.name: ownerId
parameters.description: The ID of the workspace to return logs for

parameters.name: direction
parameters.description:
The direction to query logs for. Backward will return most recent logs first.
Forward will start with the oldest logs in the time range.

parameters.name: resource
parameters.description: Filter logs by their resource. A resource is the id of a server, cronjob, job, postgres, redis, or workflow.

parameters.name: instance
parameters.description: Filter logs by the instance they were emitted from. An instance is the id of a specific running server.

parameters.name: host
parameters.description: Filter request logs by their host. [Wildcards and regex](https://render.com/docs/logging#wildcards-and-regular-expressions) are supported.

parameters.name: statusCode
parameters.description: Filter request logs by their status code. [Wildcards and regex](https://render.com/docs/logging#wildcards-and-regular-expressions) are supported.

parameters.name: method
parameters.description: Filter request logs by their requests method. [Wildcards and regex](https://render.com/docs/logging#wildcards-and-regular-expressions) are supported.

parameters.name: task
parameters.description: Filter logs by their task(s)

parameters.name: taskRun
parameters.description: Filter logs by their task run id(s)

parameters.name: level
parameters.description: Filter logs by their severity level. [Wildcards and regex](https://render.com/docs/logging#wildcards-and-regular-expressions) are supported.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Filter logs by their type. Types include `app` for application logs, `request` for request logs, and `build` for build logs. You can find the full set of types available for a query by using the `GET /logs/values` endpoint.

parameters.name: text
parameters.description: Filter by the text of the logs. [Wildcards and regex](https://render.com/docs/logging#wildcards-and-regular-expressions) are supported.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: Filter request logs by their path. [Wildcards and regex](https://render.com/docs/logging#wildcards-and-regular-expressions) are supported.

cursorParam.name: cursor
cursorParam.description: The position in the result list to start from when fetching paginated results. For details, see [Pagination](https://api-docs.render.com/reference/pagination).

limitParam.name: limit
limitParam.description: The maximum number of items to return. For details, see [Pagination](https://api-docs.render.com/reference/pagination).

ownerIdParam.name: ownerId
ownerIdParam.description: The ID of the workspaces to return resources for

startTimeParam.name: startTime
startTimeParam.description: Epoch/Unix timestamp of start of time range to return. Defaults to `now() - 1 hour`.

endTimeParam.name: endTime
endTimeParam.description: Epoch/Unix timestamp of end of time range to return. Defaults to `now()`.

envParam.name: env
envParam.description: Filter for environments (runtimes) of services (deprecated; use `runtime` instead)

suspendedParam.name: suspended
suspendedParam.description: Filter resources based on whether they're suspended or not suspended

createdBeforeParam.name: createdBefore
createdBeforeParam.description: Filter for resources created before a certain time (specified as an ISO 8601 timestamp)

createdAfterParam.name: createdAfter
createdAfterParam.description: Filter for resources created after a certain time (specified as an ISO 8601 timestamp)

updatedBeforeParam.name: updatedBefore
updatedBeforeParam.description: Filter for resources updated before a certain time (specified as an ISO 8601 timestamp)

updatedAfterParam.name: updatedAfter
updatedAfterParam.description: Filter for resources updated after a certain time (specified as an ISO 8601 timestamp)

environmentIdParam.name: environmentId
environmentIdParam.description: Filter for resources that belong to an environment

dashboardUrl.description: The URL to view the service in the Render Dashboard

registryCredentialRegistry.description: The registry to use this credential with

username.description: The username associated with the credential

updatedAt.description: Last updated time for the credential

ownerId.description: The ID of the owner for this image. This should match the owner of the service as well as the owner of any specified registry credential.

registryCredentialId.description: Optional reference to the registry credential passed to the image repository to retrieve this image.

imagePath.description: Path to the image used for this server (e.g docker.io/library/nginx:latest).

uri.description: The page to be served when [maintenance mode](https://render.com/docs/maintenance-mode) is enabled. When empty, the default maintenance mode page is served.

maxShutdownDelaySeconds.description: The maximum amount of time (in seconds) that Render waits for your application process to exit gracefully after sending it a SIGTERM signal.

min.description: The minimum number of instances for the service

max.description: The maximum number of instances for the service

percentage.description: Determines when your service will be scaled. If the average resource utilization is significantly above/below the target, we will increase/decrease the number of instances.
Determines when your service will be scaled. If the average resource utilization is significantly above/below the target, we will increase/decrease the number of instances.

numInstances.description: For a *manually* scaled service, this is the number of instances the service is scaled to. DOES NOT indicate the number of running instances for an *autoscaled* service.

min.description: The minimum number of instances for the service

max.description: The maximum number of instances for the service

percentage.description: Determines when your service will be scaled. If the average resource utilization is significantly above/below the target, we will increase/decrease the number of instances.
Determines when your service will be scaled. If the average resource utilization is significantly above/below the target, we will increase/decrease the number of instances.

numInstances.description: For a *manually* scaled service, this is the number of instances the service is scaled to. DOES NOT indicate the number of running instances for an *autoscaled* service.

min.description: The minimum number of instances for the service

max.description: The maximum number of instances for the service

percentage.description: Determines when your service will be scaled. If the average resource utilization is significantly above/below the target, we will increase/decrease the number of instances.
Determines when your service will be scaled. If the average resource utilization is significantly above/below the target, we will increase/decrease the number of instances.

numInstances.description: For a *manually* scaled service, this is the number of instances the service is scaled to. DOES NOT indicate the number of running instances for an *autoscaled* service.

name.description: The service's name. Must be unique within the workspace.

ownerId.description: The ID of the workspace the service belongs to. Obtain your workspace's ID from its Settings page in the Render Dashboard.

repo.description: The service's repository URL. Do not specify a branch in this string (use the `branch` parameter instead).

branch.description: The repo branch to pull, build, and deploy. If omitted, uses the repository's default branch.

generateValue.description: If true, Render generates a strong random value for this environment variable on creation. Cannot be combined with `value`.

environmentId.description: The ID of the environment the service belongs to, if any. Obtain an environment's ID from its Settings page in the Render Dashboard.

min.description: The minimum number of instances for the service

max.description: The maximum number of instances for the service

percentage.description: Determines when your service will be scaled. If the average resource utilization is significantly above/below the target, we will increase/decrease the number of instances.
Determines when your service will be scaled. If the average resource utilization is significantly above/below the target, we will increase/decrease the number of instances.

plan.description: The instance type to use. If omitted, defaults to `starter` when creating a new service.

min.description: The minimum number of instances for the service

max.description: The maximum number of instances for the service

percentage.description: Determines when your service will be scaled. If the average resource utilization is significantly above/below the target, we will increase/decrease the number of instances.
Determines when your service will be scaled. If the average resource utilization is significantly above/below the target, we will increase/decrease the number of instances.

min.description: The minimum number of instances for the service

max.description: The maximum number of instances for the service

percentage.description: Determines when your service will be scaled. If the average resource utilization is significantly above/below the target, we will increase/decrease the number of instances.
Determines when your service will be scaled. If the average resource utilization is significantly above/below the target, we will increase/decrease the number of instances.

plan.description: The instance type to use. Legacy variants (`*_legacy`) identify grandfathered plans no longer offered for new services. Note that base services on any paid instance type can't create preview instances with the `free` instance type.

path.description: The request path to add the header to. Wildcards will cause headers to be applied to all matching paths.

twoFactorAuthEnabled.description: Whether two-factor authentication is enabled for the owner. Only present if `type` is `user`.

priority.description: Redirect and Rewrite Rules are applied in priority order starting at 0
Redirect and Rewrite Rules are applied in priority order starting at 0. Defaults to last in the priority list.
Redirect and Rewrite Rules are applied in priority order starting at 0. Moves this route to the specified priority and adjusts other route priorities accordingly.

serviceEnv.description: This field has been deprecated, runtime should be used in its place.

sshAddress.description: The SSH address for the service. Only present for services that have SSH enabled.

image.description: Image information used when creating the deploy. Not present for Git-backed deploys

ref.description: Image reference used when creating the deploy

sha.description: SHA that the image reference was resolved to when creating the deploy

registryCredential.description: Name of credential used to pull the image, if provided

pullRequestPreviewsEnabled.description: This field has been deprecated. previews.generation should be used in its place.

renderSubdomainPolicy.description: Controls whether render.com subdomains are available for the service

description.description: User-provided description of the CIDR block

DeployMode.description:
Controls deployment behavior when triggering a deploy.

- `deploy_only`: Deploy the last successful build without rebuilding (minimizes downtime)
- `build_and_deploy`: Build new code and deploy it (default behavior when not specified)

**Note:** `deploy_only` cannot be combined with `commitId`, `imageUrl` or `clearCache` parameters,
as those are build related fields.

datadogAPIKey.description: The Datadog API key for the Datadog agent to monitor the new database.

datadogSite.description: Datadog region to use for monitoring the new database. Defaults to 'US1'.

name.description: The name of the database as it will appear in the Render Dashboard

ownerId.description: The ID of the workspace to create the database for

diskSizeGB.description: The number of gigabytes of disk space to allocate for the database

name.description: The display name of the replica instance.
The display name of the replica instance.

expiresAt.description: The time at which the database will be expire. Applies to free tier databases only.

dashboardUrl.description: The URL to view the Postgres instance in the Render Dashboard

expiresAt.description: The time at which the database will be expire. Applies to free tier databases only.

dashboardUrl.description: The URL to view the Postgres instance in the Render Dashboard

pendingMaintenanceBy.description: If present, the maintenance run cannot be scheduled for later than this date-time.

paidPlan.description: Defaults to `starter` when creating a new database.

400BadRequest.description: The request could not be understood by the server.

401Unauthorized.description: Authorization information is missing or invalid.

402PaymentRequired.description: You must enter payment information to perform this request.

403Forbidden.description: You do not have permissions for the requested resource.

404NotFound.description: Unable to find the requested resource.

406NotAcceptable.description: Unable to generate preferred media types as specified by Accept request header.

409Conflict.description: The current state of the resource conflicts with this request.

410Gone.description: The requested resource is no longer available.

500InternalServerError.description: An unexpected server error has occurred.

Logs200Response.description: A collection of logs with pagination metadata

schema.description: A run of a cron job

hasMore.description: True if there are more logs to fetch

nextStartTime.description: The start time to use in the next query to fetch the next set of logs

nextEndTime.description: The end time to use in the next query to fetch the next set of logs

id.description: A unique ID of the log entry

message.description: The message of the log entry

timestamp.description: The timestamp of the log entry

name.description: The name of the log label

value.description: The value of the log label
